prose:
Computed HOMO–LUMO gap of triplet O₂

Method provenance
• Property: HOMO–LUMO energy gap (Koopmans orbital difference) in eV  
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF (UKS)  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied fixed geometry (`/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz`); single-point only (no re-optimization)  
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)  
• Charge 0, multiplicity 3 (triplet)  
• SCF convergence: converged (7 cycles)  
• Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine  
• Live log: streamed to `frontier-orbitals_<timestamp>.out`; result JSON in `o2_frontier_dft.json`

Headline numbers
HOMO energy  = −8.8739616 eV  
LUMO energy  = −3.5859031 eV  
HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.2880586 eV

Caveats
1. Orbital (Koopmans) gaps are not excitation energies; quantitative deviations from experiment expected.
2. Gas-phase, single-conformer, implicit-only environment; no temperature or vibronic effects included.

Reproducibility
Command (from `cli_invocation`):
`chemkit frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 3 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz --out o2_frontier_dft.json`  
Files: see paths above for JSON and live `.out` log.

References
1. Bannwarth, C.; Ehlert, S.; Grimme, S. *J. Chem. Theory Comput.* **2019**, *15*, 1652–1671. https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.8b01176.
2. Sun, Q.; et al. *J. Chem. Phys.* **2020**, *153*, 024109. https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0006074.
3. Becke, A. D. *J. Chem. Phys.* **1993**, *98*, 5648–5652. https://doi.org/10.1063/1.464913.
4. Weigend, F.; Ahlrichs, R. *Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys.* **2005**, *7*, 3297–3305. https://doi.org/10.1039/B508541A.
